page: Microsoft Azure D48alds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Daldv6 family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at 3.7 GHz, featuring 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 2835 GB, and the instance supports Premium Storage. No GPU accelerators are present on this virtual machine.

Benchmark data indicates strong compute capabilities. The single-core stress-ng performance ranks in the top 10%, while multi-core performance falls in the top 25%. Geekbench multi-core scores also reach the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth tests show strong performance for small cached blocks, but drop to average levels for larger uncached blocks. Cryptographic and compression workloads, such as bzip3 decompression and PassMark encryption tests, consistently register in the top 25%. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference text generation with small models achieves top-tier speeds, though performance degrades to the bottom 25% when executing text generation on large 70B models.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the D48alds_v6 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive databases or massive in-memory datasets. However, this resource distribution offers high compute density and qualitative cost efficiency for CPU-bound workloads that do not require extensive RAM. The substantial 2835 GB local storage capacity combined with strong multi-core processing makes this instance well-suited for software compilation, ray tracing, batch processing, and localized database operations that fit within the 96.0 GB memory envelope.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48alds_v6 is a compute-oriented x86_64 server instance powered by a dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores, 96.0 GB of system memory, and 2835 GB of local storage. With a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this instance is optimized for compute-heavy tasks rather than memory-intensive applications. Benchmark results place its single-core and multi-core performance in the top 10% and top 25% respectively, with strong results in software compilation, ray tracing, and small-model LLM inference. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks remains average, and text generation for large 70B LLM models is a notable bottleneck. The D48alds_v6 is highly cost-efficient for high-density CPU workloads, making it suitable for software development pipelines, batch processing, and localized database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48alds_v6 is an x86_64 server featuring 48 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, optimized for high-density compute workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48alds_v6 is a compute-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage on an AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring forty-eight vCPUs, fast local storage, and optimized performance for CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
Equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory and 2835 GB of local storage
Top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core optimized for compute-heavy tasks
Best suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and batch processing workloads
